message:
fix(process): make Command methods synchronous and improve Stdio API

This commit fixes compilation errors and improves the process module API:

## Breaking Changes

- Renamed `Stdio::Piped` to `Stdio::Pipe` for consistency with standard
  library naming conventions (`std::process::Stdio::piped()`)
- Made `Command::output()`, `Command::status()`, `Child::wait()`, and
  `Child::wait_with_output()` synchronous (removed async/await)

## Rationale for Synchronous API

The previous async implementation was misleading - it used `block_on`
internally anyway. Until we have proper non-blocking waitpid integration
with the reactor, these methods are inherently blocking. Making them
explicitly synchronous:

1. Eliminates the false impression that they're non-blocking
2. Simplifies the API - callers don't need an async runtime for basic
   process spawning
3. Matches common use patterns in CLIs and build scripts
4. Avoids the overhead of async machinery for blocking operations

The TODO comment documents the path to true async: integrate with the
reactor using non-blocking waitpid when available.

## New Features

- Added `Default` impl for `Stdio` that returns `Stdio::Inherit`,
  matching typical command-line tool behavior where child processes
  inherit the parent's stdio by default
- Added `From<Stdio> for std_process::Stdio` conversion impl for
  easier interop with the standard library

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed `Child::wait()` to properly take ownership of the inner process
  handle and return a clearer error message ("process already consumed"
  instead of "child already waited")

## Test Updates

- Updated all process tests to use the synchronous API
- Removed `futures_lite::future::block_on` wrappers that are no longer
  needed
- Changed test sleep duration from 100s to 10s for faster test execution
  in the kill test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
